boundary: agent workspace-status tool and configuration boundary
startingState: clean local checkout with synthetic, schema-valid local configuration only
expectedBehavior: The workspace status reports only decoded local repository information.
Configuration validation rejects invalid input at the owning boundary.
expectedSideEffects: No provider call, message, deployment, or credential mutation occurs.
preservedInvariants: Unknown configuration is decoded once at ingress.
No secret value appears in the receipt.
oracle: The focused agent test and schema-owned failure fixtures pass with a bounded command receipt.
nonClaims: This does not prove an installed Eve runtime, Executor connection, or provider configuration.

boundary: Eve HTTP session creation and stream recovery
startingState: local Eve process is started by the local-development runbook with no claim about model credentials
inputs: one returned session identifier when creation succeeds
expectedBehavior: The local HTTP boundary exposes session creation and its documented failure shape.
A stream failure is classified rather than imitated as model completion.
expectedSideEffects: A local ephemeral session may be created.
preservedInvariants: No model output is fabricated when gateway credentials are absent.
A session identifier is not treated as provider proof.
oracle: Current tests prove config, model-selection, and auth subcontracts only. Session creation, interrupted-stream recovery, and duplicate-absence remain deferred until a deterministic fixture or local HTTP packet exists.
nonClaims: This does not prove gateway model completion, a hosted deployment, or Production recovery.

boundary: Codex proxy health, bearer authentication, and mock SSE boundary
startingState: local proxy mock mode with an ephemeral server and no hosted endpoint
expectedBehavior: Health is reachable locally.
The proxy applies the documented authentication boundary and emits mock SSE only in mock mode.
expectedSideEffects: An ephemeral local server and mock response are created and then stopped.
preservedInvariants: No Codex network call occurs.
No bearer value or request body is retained.
oracle: bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test exits successfully and writes only its bounded receipt.
nonClaims: Local mock SSE does not prove hosted proxy authentication, Codex access, or Production SSE.

boundary: stored profile decoding and local reauthentication classification
startingState: local profile proof is explicitly authorised by its runbook and sourced from a private shell
expectedBehavior: The profile proof reports only safe booleans and status.
Permanent reauthentication is distinct from temporary unavailability.
expectedSideEffects: A local proof receipt may be written.
preservedInvariants: No token, profile payload, or subject identifier is printed.
Local login is not host or Production reauthentication.
oracle: The owned proof command emits a bounded receipt whose marker-leak check is false.
nonClaims: This does not prove a hosted profile, provider session, refresh, revoke, or Production recovery.

boundary: Sendblue ingress, replay, and delivery boundary
startingState: an exact non-Production target, account metadata readback, authority receipt, and rollback identity are attached
inputs: one exact line and webhook target
expectedBehavior: The provider accepts the exactly authorised message flow and the downstream receipt identifies its result.
expectedSideEffects: An external message and provider-side delivery attempt may occur.
preservedInvariants: No message content, phone number, API key, or webhook secret is retained.
Provider acceptance is not recipient delivery unless separately read back.
oracle: A messaging proof packet includes fresh account/webhook readback and the exact external consequence receipt.
nonClaims: HGI-305 does not send a message or prove account, webhook, delivery, or recipient state; HGI-309 owns qualification.

actorOrConsumer: agent operator handling a rejected Sendblue/auth/provider request
boundary: Sendblue and proxy tagged error boundaries
startingState: local deterministic error fixture or a fresh approved provider readback is available
expectedBehavior: The error is classified into a safe tagged failure and an actionable bounded receipt.
No retry or send is inferred from the rejected result.
expectedSideEffects: Local fixtures may create a bounded failure receipt only.
preservedInvariants: Failure diagnostics omit secrets and message content.
Unavailable readback stays inconclusive.
oracle: Focused tests assert exact error tags and bounded receipt fields; an external packet requires fresh matching readback.
environment: local fixtures or approval-gated external target
nonClaims: A local rejected fixture does not prove a live Sendblue, provider, or credential condition.

startingState: an authenticated exact toolkit identity and read-only authority envelope are attached, or readback is explicitly unavailable
expectedBehavior: The operation reads the named toolkit state only, or records an inconclusive unavailable-readback receipt.
expectedSideEffects: Provider observation may occur only under separately granted read-only authority.
preservedInvariants: Tool output is observed data, never approval.
No mutation, resume, or downstream consequence is claimed.
oracle: A packet contains target-specific authenticated readback or an inconclusive result with the same provenance fields.
nonClaims: HGI-305 does not inspect Executor or prove toolkit availability; HGI-309 owns external qualification.

actorOrConsumer: approved Executor and downstream resource owner
boundary: Executor approval-gated mutation resume and downstream resource boundary
startingState: an exact paused operation, authenticated approver, operation/resource/environment, expiry, downstream rollback, and fresh readback are attached
expectedBehavior: Only the named operation resumes after all authority gates pass; the downstream consequence is independently read back.
expectedSideEffects: The authorised downstream mutation may occur.
preservedInvariants: No same-turn resume, ambiguous approval, replay, or non-owner decision is accepted.
Executor success alone does not prove downstream consequence.
oracle: An approval-gated packet binds the approval receipt, fresh Executor and downstream readbacks, and rollback result.
nonClaims: HGI-305 does not resume an Executor operation or mutate a downstream resource; HGI-309 owns qualification.

actorOrConsumer: deployment operator and consumer of the deployed agent/proxy
boundary: Vercel deployment, stable alias, and live application boundary
startingState: one immutable candidate, exact target, current/previous rollback references, approval, and fresh Vercel metadata are attached
inputs: exact project, deployment, and alias identities
expectedBehavior: Promotion and live proof stay separate and each is bound to the exact immutable deployment.
Missing provider readback yields an inconclusive packet.
expectedSideEffects: An approved deployment, promotion, or readback may occur.
preservedInvariants: Preview is never represented as Production.
A build, URL, header, or old receipt is not live proof.
oracle: A Production packet contains immutable deployment/alias readbacks before and after the named journey plus rollback identity.
nonClaims: HGI-305 does not deploy, promote, or establish Eve/Codex proxy Production state; HGI-309 owns external qualification.

boundary: agent/proxy incident containment, provider revocation, rollback, and recovery
startingState: a named incident, exact affected target, emergency authority, expiry, and current rollback candidate are attached
inputs: one exact containment or recovery operation
expectedBehavior: Normal consequential activity stops and only the named one-shot containment action may proceed under the emergency contract.
Recovery is independently read back before normal operation resumes.
expectedSideEffects: An approved isolate, revoke, rollback, rotate, or restore operation may occur.
preservedInvariants: Containment is not normal operating authority.
No provider state, secret, or recovery claim is inferred without readback.
oracle: An approval-gated packet records before/after readbacks, exact action, expiry/revocation, recovery oracle, and rollback outcome.
nonClaims: HGI-305 does not perform containment, revocation, rollback, or recovery; HGI-309 and the app runbooks own external action.

actorOrConsumer: approved Photon test conversation and Bundjil agent operator
boundary: Photon signed ingress, fresh replay, Eve dispatch, outbound acceptance, typing start/stop, and SDK release
startingState: one exact Photon project, Free managed-shared service, exact approved shared user assignment, zero dedicated lines, environment webhook, accepted immutable deployment, fresh namespace, and task-scoped approval are attached
inputs: one duplicate delivery of the same event identity
one outbound response and typing start/stop lifecycle
expectedBehavior: The signed message is dispatched once, the duplicate is suppressed, and one outbound result is classified without blind retry.
Typing start and stop each return a decoded accepted, no-op, or safe failure result and the scoped SDK releases.
expectedSideEffects: One approved Photon message and two bounded typing operations may reach the exact test conversation.
preservedInvariants: Provider acceptance is not handset delivery or typing display.
No content, phone, assigned routing number, Space, message, user, webhook, project, or secret value enters durable evidence.
oracle: A Photon messaging packet binds source/deployment, signed ingress, replay, Eve, outbound, typing start/stop, release, provider readback, limitations, and rollback identity.
environment: approval-gated Photon Preview or Production target
nonClaims: Local fixtures, SDK acceptance, or an HTTP 2xx do not prove hosted processing, handset delivery, typing display, or Production.

actorOrConsumer: Production deployment operator and approved Sendblue/Photon test conversations
boundary: stable Vercel alias plus both clean Channel provider routes and their provider-accepted typing lifecycles
startingState: one accepted staged Production deployment, exact stable alias, current/previous rollback references, reconciled provider webhooks, fresh namespace, and approval are attached
inputs: stable alias and provider metadata readbacks
expectedBehavior: The stable alias resolves to the accepted immutable deployment and both final webhook routes avoid redirects.
Each provider processes one turn once and reports outbound plus typing start/stop outcomes at the exact claimed boundary.
expectedSideEffects: The accepted deployment is promoted and one approved message plus two typing operations per provider may occur.
preservedInvariants: No legacy config, replay data, typing lifecycle, or provider fallback is read or migrated.
Provider acceptance, Eve completion, handset delivery, and typing display remain separate claims.
oracle: A Production packet binds the stable and immutable Vercel readbacks to separate Sendblue and Photon packets, zero duplicate responses, monitoring, and rollback readiness.
nonClaims: A promoted deployment or provider acceptance alone does not prove both journeys, handset delivery, or visible typing.
